title: Parallel R notebooks for shipped datasets
context: The primary notebook format is Python (Jupyter), but biostatistics audiences (especially those learning survival analysis, Cox models, and epidemiology) commonly use R (survival, tidyverse, limma, DESeq2). If a confirmed adoption partner has an R-using audience, parallel R Markdown notebooks for shipped datasets significantly widen adoption. The backlog note specifies 'if partner audience uses R (biostatistics)' — this task is contingent on partner confirmation. Each R notebook must include the safety notice in the first chunk and be tested to run without errors. MIT licensed.
objective: Produce parallel R Markdown notebooks for >= 2 shipped datasets (contingent on partner-confirmed R audience), each including the safety notice and running without errors in a standard R environment.
acceptanceCriteria: Contingency confirmed: an adoption partner with an R-using audience has been identified (partner-016 or subsequent partner).
R Markdown notebooks produced for >= 2 shipped datasets (priority: pilot-008 survival analysis, ds-expr-013 differential expression — both have strong R ecosystems).
Each notebook: first chunk contains the mandatory safety notice; runs without errors in a standard R environment (survival, ggplot2, limma or equivalent); exercises equivalent to the Python version.
Notebooks committed to the project repo alongside the Python versions; MIT licensed.
Technical reviewer confirms notebooks run correctly; educator reviewer confirms R exercises are equivalent to Python exercises in pedagogical quality.
pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass for any tooling changes; commit DCO signed-off.
resources: PLAN.md (§Scope — notebooks in Python; R where audience benefits; §Open questions — Python vs R)
output: A merged PR adding parallel R Markdown notebooks for >= 2 shipped datasets, with safety notices and confirmed to run without errors.
